articles.title: J&K announces summer vacation for Jammu schools from June 1 amid intense heatwave - DD News
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Jammu and Kashmir
articles.full_text:
The Jammu and Kashmir government on Saturday announced summer vacation for schools in the Jammu division in view of the prevailing heatwave conditions in the region.
Jammu and Kashmir education minister Sakina Itoo said on X that summer vacations for schools falling under the Summer Zone of Jammu Division will begin from June 1 for primary classes and from June 8 for students of Classes 6 to 12.
“Keeping in view the prevailing weather conditions, summer vacations for schools falling under Summer Zone of Jammu Division shall commence with effect from June 1 for Primary Classes and with effect from June 8 for students of Classes 6 to 12. Wishing all students a safe and enjoyable vacation,” she posted.
Meanwhile, the Jammu Municipal Corporation (JMC) issued a heatwave advisory after temperatures in the city were forecast to touch 45 degrees Celsius. Authorities urged people, especially children and elderly citizens, to remain indoors and avoid unnecessary movement during peak afternoon hours.
The civic body said prolonged exposure to heat could lead to dehydration, exhaustion and heat-related illnesses. Water sprinkling vehicles have been deployed in several parts of the city to reduce surface temperatures and provide temporary relief to commuters and pedestrians.
The JMC advised residents to stay hydrated, wear light cotton clothes, use umbrellas or head coverings while stepping outdoors and avoid direct exposure to sunlight. People were also asked to keep homes properly ventilated and consume adequate fluids during the extreme weather conditions.
Special caution has been advised for children, elderly citizens and those suffering from health complications. Authorities also warned residents against leaving children or elderly persons inside parked vehicles, saying enclosed vehicles can become dangerously hot within minutes.
Officials said field workers and sanitation staff working outdoors have been instructed to take regular hydration breaks and follow precautionary measures.
According to data released by the Meteorological Centre Srinagar, Srinagar recorded a maximum temperature of 23.9 degrees Celsius on Saturday, 1.5 degrees below normal for this time of the year, while Jammu recorded 39.9 degrees Celsius, 1.5 degrees above normal.
-IANS

articles.title: Summer Vacations 2026: Jammu division, Ghaziabad schools closed amid rising temperatures - Careers360
articles.search_term: heatwave (Anantnag OR Bandipore OR Baramulla OR Budgam OR Doda OR Ganderbal OR Jammu OR Kathua OR Kishtwar OR Kulgam OR Kupwara OR Poonch OR Pulwama OR Rajouri OR Ramban OR Reasi OR Samba OR Shopian OR Srinagar OR Udhampur) Jammu and Kashmir
articles.full_text:
Summer Vacations 2026: Jammu division, Ghaziabad schools closed amid rising temperatures
Heatwave impact on schools: summer vacations extended in several states as temperatures soar across north India.
The Jammu and Kashmir government has announced summer vacations for schools in the summer zone of Jammu division. Considering heatwave conditions, the schools will remain closed from June 1. Education minister Sakina Itoo took to X to announce this. "Considering the prevailing weather conditions, summer vacations for schools in the Summer Zone of Jammu Division shall commence from June 1, 2026, for primary classes and on June 8, 2026, for students in Classes 6th to 12th," the minister said.
The Jammu region has been witnessing severe heatwave conditions over the past few days, with several districts recording unusually high temperatures. According to officials, Samba district touched nearly 45 degrees Celsius, while Jammu recorded around 43 degrees Celsius, causing hardship for residents, especially school students. The minister also extended wishes to students for a safe and pleasant summer vacation and advised them to follow necessary precautions during the break.

Amid severe heatwave conditions in Ghaziabad, the district administration on Thursday announced summer vacations for all schools across different boards from May 23, citing concerns over students’ health and safety. The decision was taken following directions from the district magistrate, who also urged residents to follow advisories issued by the India Meteorological Department (IMD) to stay protected from extreme heat.
Ghaziabad schools closed amid heatwave
With the latest order, all schools affiliated with the Basic Education Council and other education boards, including private institutions, will remain closed for summer vacations from May 23. However, the administration clarified that CBSE board examinations scheduled during this period will continue as planned. Parents across Ghaziabad welcomed the administration’s decision, saying children were struggling to cope with the intense heat during school hours. Many said students were returning home exhausted despite carrying water bottles, caps, and towels to school every day.
In neighbouring Noida, although no official district-wide order has been issued so far, most schools have already announced summer vacations starting May 22. Earlier, on May 20, only government primary schools had been directed to close due to the heatwave. The IMD has predicted that heatwave conditions are likely to continue in several parts of western Uttar Pradesh over the next few days.

Delhi-NCR schools set for extended summer vacation
According to multiple media reports, schools across Delhi-NCR are likely to remain closed till June 30, with classes expected to resume on July 1. This will result in nearly 50 to 57 days of summer vacation in several schools. While summer holidays have already begun in Delhi government schools, remedial classes for students of Classes 9, 10, and 12 are continuing in limited morning sessions.
Summer vacations in Bihar will be observed from June 1 to June 20, while schools in Telangana and Andhra Pradesh remain closed from April 24 to June 11. In Chhattisgarh, summer holidays are scheduled from April 20 to June 15. Odisha schools began vacations from April 27, whereas in several districts of West Bengal, summer holidays have been extended till May 31.
Follow us for the latest education news on colleges and universities, admission, courses, exams, research, education policies, study abroad and more..
To get in touch, write to us at news@careers360.com.

articles.title: Heatwave causes premature drying of blooms at Jakhani Park in Jammu and Kashmir - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
A sudden increase in temperatures has adversely affected the floral displays at Jakhani Park, a key tourist location in Udhampur, Jammu and Kashmir. The early heatwave has caused the park’s hybrid seasonal flowers to dry up well before their expected lifecycle, reducing the duration of their bloom.
Situated along the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway, Jakhani Park serves as a major recreational spot and offers visual respite to thousands of travellers heading towards Patnitop, Srinagar, and other regional destinations.
Assistant Floriculture Officer Pawan Kumar explained the impact of the unusual weather pattern on the garden, stating, “Due to the recent temperature rise, the seasonal blooms that we plant are getting affected. The general longevity of these flowers would be till the end of May, but due to the heat, they are drying up prematurely.”
In response, the Floriculture Department has modified its maintenance regimen to safeguard the remaining plants and preserve the park’s aesthetic appeal. Watering schedules have been shifted to evening hours to minimise evaporation and cool the soil. These measures aim to extend the life of the current flowers so that visitors can continue to enjoy the display.
Preparations are also underway for the upcoming seasonal transition. Kumar said, “Fifteen to twenty days from now, we will plant the new seasonal seedlings.” The department intends to introduce heat-adapted seedlings better suited to the current climatic conditions.
Despite the detrimental effects of daytime heat on the flora, Jakhani Park maintains its popularity. Recent recreational enhancements, including the installation of children’s equipment, have increased visitor numbers, especially in the evenings. Kumar noted, “We have installed children’s equipment that has pushed the footfall, especially in evenings,” adding that families and tourists continue to visit in large numbers to enjoy the cooler hours.

articles.title: Jammu summer vacation 2026 announced for schools amid rising heatwave conditions - Zee News
articles.full_text:
Jammu summer vacation 2026 announced for schools amid rising heatwave conditions
Jammu summer vacation 2026: According to the official announcement, summer vacations for primary classes will begin from June 1, 2026. Students studying in Classes 6 to 12 will start their summer break from June 8, 2026.
Trending Photos
Jammu summer vacation 2026: The Jammu and Kashmir government has announced summer vacations for schools falling under the Summer Zone of Jammu Division in view of the prevailing heatwave conditions and rising temperatures across the region.

Education Minister Sakina Itoo shared the update through an official post on social media, outlining separate vacation schedules for primary and senior classes.
Summer vacation dates for Jammu summer zone schools
According to the official announcement, summer vacations for primary classes will begin from June 1, 2026. Students studying in Classes 6 to 12 will start their summer break from June 8, 2026. The vacation period for both groups is scheduled to continue until July 22, 2026.
The staggered schedule has been introduced keeping in mind the extreme heat conditions affecting the Jammu region.
Sakina Itoo shares official announcement
Education Minister Sakina Itoo announced the decision through her official X account, stating that the move was taken considering the prevailing weather conditions in the Summer Zone of Jammu Division. (X (formerly Twitter))
In her message, the minister also wished students a safe and enjoyable summer vacation.
Keeping in view the prevailing weather conditions, summer vacations for schools falling under Summer Zone of Jammu Division shall commence w.e.f. 01.06.2026 for Primary Classes and w.e.f. 08.06.2026 for students of Classes 6th to 12th.
Wishing all students a safe and enjoyable… — Sakina Itoo (@sakinaitoo) May 23, 2026
Heatwave conditions prompt government action
The decision comes as Jammu continues to experience unusually high temperatures. According to reports, the city recorded a maximum temperature of 39.9 degrees Celsius, which was above the seasonal average. Authorities have warned that temperatures could rise further in the coming days.
The Jammu Municipal Corporation has also issued a heatwave advisory asking residents, especially children and elderly citizens, to avoid unnecessary outdoor movement during peak afternoon hours.

Advisory issued for students and parents
Authorities have advised parents to ensure children remain hydrated and avoid prolonged exposure to heat during the vacation period. The administration has also cautioned people against leaving children inside parked vehicles due to rapidly increasing temperatures inside enclosed spaces.
The precautionary measures are aimed at protecting students from heat-related illnesses during the ongoing summer season.
Schools in other regions may follow a different schedule
The vacation announcement currently applies only to schools falling under the Summer Zone of Jammu Division. Educational institutions in the Kashmir Division and winter zones of Jammu may follow separate schedules depending on weather conditions in their respective regions.
Earlier as well, school timings and reopening schedules in Jammu and Kashmir were revised due to changing weather conditions and heatwave concerns.
Several states across India have either revised school timings or announced early summer vacations due to severe heatwave conditions this year. Educational authorities in different regions have been taking preventive measures to ensure student safety amid rising temperatures.
Live Tv

articles.title: Jammu Weather Update: Heatwave Pushes Temperature to 40°C, Rain Chances Persist Amid Humid Conditions - LatestLY
articles.full_text:
Jammu Weather Update: Heatwave Pushes Temperature to 40°C, Rain Chances Persist Amid Humid Conditions
Jammu continues to reel under severe heatwave conditions on May 24, 2026, even as isolated pre-monsoon activity triggers brief spells of rain in some areas. According to the regional Meteorological Centre, the city is expected to witness a maximum daytime temperature of 40°C under largely sunny skies, with unstable atmospheric conditions increasing the possibility of sudden localized showers.
Jammu continues to reel under severe heatwave conditions on May 24, 2026, even as isolated pre-monsoon activity triggers brief spells of rain in some areas. According to the regional Meteorological Centre, the city is expected to witness a maximum daytime temperature of 40°C under largely sunny skies, with unstable atmospheric conditions increasing the possibility of sudden localized showers.
As of mid-morning, Jammu recorded a temperature of 36°C accompanied by light rainfall in parts of the region. The humidity level stood at 24%, creating warm and heavy weather conditions despite intermittent rain activity. Officials also noted a moderate UV index of 5 during the morning hours. Jammu and Kashmir Weather Alert: MeT Forecasts Erratic Rain and Snow From April 3 to 5, Peak Activity on April 4.
Meteorological tracking systems indicate a 45% probability of localized daytime rain developing across parts of Jammu later in the day. Winds are currently blowing from the northeast at around 3 mph. Weather officials further stated that there remains a 29% chance of scattered showers continuing during the first half of the day before intense heat radiation dominates afternoon conditions.
Jammu Weather Today, May 24
Jammu Night Weather Forecast
For the evening and nighttime period, the weather department has predicted mostly cloudy skies across Jammu. Temperatures are expected to remain high, with the minimum temperature likely to settle around 28°C, offering limited relief from the ongoing heatwave. Delhi Rains-Weather Forecast: Rainfall Brings Brief Relief to Delhiites Amid Heatwave; IMD Warns of Stormy Weather Across 13 States.
Rainfall chances are expected to reduce significantly after sunset, with only a 10% probability of precipitation during the night. Wind patterns are also expected to shift later in the day, with northwesterly winds likely to blow at approximately 7 mph. The average daily humidity level is expected to remain close to 23%.
Heatwave Impact in Jammu and Kashmir
The prolonged spell of extreme heat has prompted administrative action across Jammu and Kashmir. In view of the continuing heatwave conditions, educational authorities have announced revised summer vacation schedules for schools in the summer zone of the Jammu division.
Primary classes will officially close for summer vacations from June 1, 2026, while schools for Classes 6 to 12 are scheduled to shut from June 8, 2026.
Health authorities have advised residents to avoid direct exposure to sunlight during peak afternoon hours, particularly as the UV index is expected to rise sharply to a hazardous level of 10 during the day. Citizens have also been urged to remain hydrated and limit outdoor activity amid the prevailing heatwave conditions.
(The above story first appeared on LatestLY on May 24, 2026 09:50 AM IST. For more news and updates on politics, world, sports, entertainment and lifestyle, log on to our website latestly.com).

articles.title: Weather Today [24 May, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Heat Alert, Monsoon & Thunderstorms Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Hyderabad & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian
articles.full_text:
Weather Today, 24 May 2026: IMD predicts rain, thunderstorms & snowfall as a new Western Disturbance hits Northwest India. Check the full city-wise forecast for Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Hyderabad, Rajasthan, Jammu & Kashmir, Karnataka, Punjab & more. Get live temperature, AQI & rain predictions.
India Weather Update Today: India is witnessing two completely different weather systems at the same time while large parts of North, Central and Western India continue to struggle under a severe heatwave, southern and northeastern states are preparing for heavy rain as the Southwest Monsoon steadily advances. Temperatures have crossed 47°C in some regions, while several coastal and hill states are already reporting thunderstorms and intense rainfall activity.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), severe heatwave conditions are expected to continue across Northwest and Central India through the coming week and at the same time, monsoon activity over the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal is strengthening rapidly, bringing moisture-laden winds into southern India.
Higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand may witness light rain and isolated snowfall in elevated mountain areas over the next 48 hours. Srinagar remains unusually cool compared to northern plains.
Srinagar Weather: Minimum: 9°C & Maximum: 17°C
Tourist destinations in Gulmarg and Sonamarg could experience brief snowfall during late-night hours due to western disturbances interacting with cold mountain winds.
Meteorologists say multiple atmospheric factors are fueling the heat surge:
Brahmapuri in Maharashtra touched 47.2°C, making it among the hottest places in the country this week.
Health authorities warn that prolonged exposure may increase risks of dehydration, heat exhaustion and heatstroke, especially among children and elderly citizens.
Yes, the Southwest Monsoon has advanced into:
The IMD expects further advancement into Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and northeastern states within the next 3 to 4 days.
Minimum: 30°C | Maximum: 45°C
Delhi-NCR remains under intense heatwave conditions with strong dry winds during afternoon hour and wind speeds may reach 30 km/h, while humidity remains low. Heat index values could feel even harsher during peak afternoon hours.
Minimum: 30°C | Maximum: 34°C
Chennai is expected to remain humid with chances of isolated coastal showers while sea breeze conditions may offer temporary evening relief.
Minimum: 30°C | Maximum: 33°C
Mumbai continues to experience warm and sticky weather conditions while cloud cover is expected to increase gradually as monsoon currents strengthen offshore.
Minimum: 26°C | Maximum: 41°C
Hyderabad remains hot and dry with partly cloudy skies and afternoon thunderstorms may develop in isolated areas.
Minimum: 23°C | Maximum: 35°C
Several districts in Karnataka may witness thunderstorms and moderate rainfall due to advancing monsoon activity while coastal Karnataka could receive heavier showers later this week.
Minimum: 29°C | Maximum: 46°C
Heatwave conditions continue across most districts including Lucknow, Kanpur and Prayagraj while hot winds locally known as “loo” are expected during daytime.
Minimum: 32°C | Maximum: 43°C
Jaipur and surrounding areas remain extremely hot and dry. Western Rajasthan may witness dust storms in isolated pockets.
Minimum: 9°C | Maximum: 17°C
Cooler temperatures persist across Kashmir Valley while cloud movement over hilly regions may trigger evening showers.
Minimum: 27°C | Maximum: 44°C
Punjab continues to face severe heat conditions, especially in Chandigarh and surrounding districts while the temperature may climb further during the week.
Minimum: 30°C | Maximum: 42°C
Ahmedabad remains under hot and dry conditions while warm nighttime temperatures are increasing discomfort levels.
Rising temperatures may affect:
According to climate studies, India experiences nearly 20% higher urban heat stress today compared to two decades ago.
Delhi’s AQI remains in the Poor category due to dust accumulation and stagnant atmospheric conditions.
1. Are There Any Travel Or Safety Warnings?
Yes. Fishermen have been advised not to venture into parts of the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal due to rough sea conditions.
2. Will Temperatures Rise Across The Country?
Northwest and Central India may see temperatures rise by 2°C to 4°C over the next few days.
3. Will There Be Any Rain Today?
Yes. Southern and northeastern states are likely to receive moderate to heavy rainfall.
4. Where Will It Snow Today?
Light snowfall is possible in isolated higher-altitude areas of Jammu and Kashmir.
5. Is Delhi Under Heatwave Alert?
Yes. Delhi remains under severe heatwave warning with temperatures nearing 45°C to 47°C.
Disclaimer: Weather forecasts are for guidance only. Conditions may change rapidly. Always check local authorities for emergencies and travel alerts.

articles.title: Jammu Division Schools To Observe Summer Vacation From June 1 Amid Heatwave Conditions - Times Now
articles.search_term: heatwave (Anantnag OR Bandipore OR Baramulla OR Budgam OR Doda OR Ganderbal OR Jammu OR Kathua OR Kishtwar OR Kulgam OR Kupwara OR Poonch OR Pulwama OR Rajouri OR Ramban OR Reasi OR Samba OR Shopian OR Srinagar OR Udhampur) Jammu and Kashmir
articles.full_text:
Summer vacations for schools in the Summer Zone of Jammu Division will begin from June 1 for primary classes and from June 8 for students of Classes 6 to 12, Education Minister Sakina Itoo announced on Saturday amid rising temperatures across the region.
Sharing the update through an official post on X, the minister said the decision had been taken in view of the prevailing weather conditions in Jammu division, where several districts have been witnessing intense heat over the past few days.
“Keeping in view the prevailing weather conditions, summer vacations for schools falling under Summer Zone of Jammu Division shall commence w.e.f. 01.06.2026 for Primary Classes and w.e.f. 08.06.2026 for students of Classes 6th to 12th,” Sakina Itoo posted on X. She also extended wishes to students for a “safe and enjoyable vacation.”
The announcement has brought relief to students and parents as temperatures in parts of Jammu division continue to remain unusually high. According to reports, districts such as Samba and Jammu city have recorded temperatures crossing 40 degrees Celsius in recent days, prompting concerns over student safety and health during school hours.
As per the schedule, vacations for primary classes will begin a week earlier than senior classes. Schools are expected to reopen after the summer break in July, although detailed reopening instructions are likely to be issued separately by the Directorate of School Education Jammu (DSEJ).
Officials have indicated that teaching and non-teaching staff may be required to report before the reopening of schools to complete preparatory work, including campus maintenance, timetable planning and academic arrangements for the new session.
The move follows similar precautionary measures adopted by several states and union territories across North India, where soaring temperatures and heatwave-like conditions have disrupted normal routines. Education departments in multiple regions have either revised school timings or announced early vacations to safeguard students from extreme weather exposure.
The Jammu and Kashmir administration is expected to issue detailed guidelines regarding school operations, remedial classes and academic activities during the vacation period in the coming days.

articles.title: Hot, Dry Weather And Heat Wave Conditions Likely In Jammu - Live India
articles.search_term: heatwave (Anantnag OR Bandipore OR Baramulla OR Budgam OR Doda OR Ganderbal OR Jammu OR Kathua OR Kishtwar OR Kulgam OR Kupwara OR Poonch OR Pulwama OR Rajouri OR Ramban OR Reasi OR Samba OR Shopian OR Srinagar OR Udhampur) Jammu and Kashmir
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department’s Meteorological Centre in Srinagar has forecast hot and dry weather conditions across Jammu and Kashmir, with isolated heat wave conditions expected in the plains of the Jammu division.
According to the latest weather advisory issued on May 18, residents in several parts of Jammu are likely to experience rising temperatures and dry conditions during the day.
Heat Wave Alert For Jammu Plains
The weather department stated that isolated areas in the plains of the Jammu division could face heat wave conditions due to persistent hot and dry weather. Authorities have advised people to stay hydrated, avoid direct sun exposure during peak afternoon hours, and take precautions against heat-related illnesses.
The advisory mainly applies to low-lying regions where temperatures are expected to remain significantly above normal levels.
Weather To Change From May 19 To 21
The Meteorological Centre said weather conditions are expected to shift between May 19 and May 21. During this period, skies across the Union Territory are likely to remain partly to generally cloudy.
Several areas may witness light rain, thundershowers, gusty winds, and possible hailstorms, especially during afternoon and evening hours. The impact is expected to be more noticeable in scattered to many locations across both Kashmir and Jammu divisions.
Thunderstorms And Gusty Winds Expected
The weather department warned that thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and strong winds reaching speeds of 40 to 50 kmph may occur at a few places between May 19 and 21.
Due to the possibility of sudden gusty winds and unstable weather conditions, authorities advised people to remain cautious while travelling or engaging in outdoor activities.
Advisory For Boating And Shikara Services
As a precautionary measure, the MET Centre advised suspension of boating and shikara riding activities, particularly during afternoon and late afternoon hours when weather disturbances are expected to intensify.
Tourists and locals visiting lakes and water bodies in Kashmir have been urged to follow official advisories and avoid unnecessary risks during changing weather conditions.
Dry Weather Likely Again After May 25
According to the forecast, weather conditions are expected to improve after May 25. Dry weather is likely to prevail across Jammu and Kashmir from May 26 to May 28, bringing more stable atmospheric conditions to the region.

articles.title: Jammu News: अरनिया अस्पताल में लू के मामले बढ़े, रोज 10 से ज्यादा मरीज पहुंच रहे - Amar Ujala
articles.full_text:
{"_id":"6a12158406cc1cbbf00859c8","slug":"jammu-kashmir-news-samba-news-c-274-1-rsp1001-102729-2026-05-24","type":"story","status":"publish","title_hn":"Jammu News: अरनिया अस्पताल में लू के मामले बढ़े, रोज 10 से ज्यादा मरीज पहुंच रहे","category":{"title":"City & states","title_hn":"शहर और राज्य","slug":"city-and-states"}}
Jammu News: अरनिया अस्पताल में लू के मामले बढ़े, रोज 10 से ज्यादा मरीज पहुंच रहे
विज्ञापन
खबरें लगातार पढ़ने के लिए अमर उजाला एप डाउनलोड करें
या
वेबसाइट पर पढ़ना जारी रखने के लिए वीडियो विज्ञापन देखें
अगर आपके पास प्रीमियम मेंबरशिप है तो
विज्ञापन
अरनिया। क्षेत्र में लू लगने के मामलों में एकदम से बढ़ोतरी हुई है। पिछले तीन दिन से अस्पताल में रोजाना स्तर पर 10 से ज्यादा मरीज लू से ग्रस्त पहुंच रहे हैं। लू के मरीजों में इजाफा होने से अस्पताल का वार्ड भरा पड़ा है। दूसरी ओर डॉक्टर मरीजों को दोपहर 12:00 बजे से शाम 4:00 बजे तक धूप में निकलने की मनाही कर रहे हैं। पर्याप्त मात्रा में पानी और तरल पदार्थ का सेवन करने के लिए कह रहे हैं।
लू के ज्यादातर मामलों में मरीज उल्टी, दस्त, थकावट और सिर चकराने के लक्षणों के साथ अस्पताल पहुंच रहे हैं। डॉक्टर जांच के बाद मरीज को दाखिल कर ग्लूकोस चढ़ा रहे हैं। डॉक्टर मरीजों को बचाव के उपाय बता रहे हैं।
लू से बचने के उपाय
दोपहर को धूप में जाने से बचें।
हल्के और सूती कपड़े पहनें।
ताजा फल, नींबू, छाछ, ओआरएस का सेवन करें
नियमित समय पर पानी का सेवन करते रहें।
बाहर निकलने से पहले सिर पर कपड़ा बांध कर रखें।
लू लगने के लक्षण
उल्टी, दस्त, सिर चकराना, पसीना, मुंह सूखना, ज्यादा प्यास लगना, थकावट महसूस होना, बेहोश होना, शरीर का तापमान बढ़ना, और सांस लेने में तकलीफ होना प्रमुख लक्षण हैं।
विज्ञापन
कोट
अस्पताल में लू के मामलों में बढ़ोतरी हुई है। भीषण गर्मी और तेज लू के कारण लोग बीमार हो रहे हैं। उन्होंने कहा कि लू से बचने का सबसे बड़ा बचाव परहेज ही है।

अस्पताल में लू के मामलों में बढ़ोतरी हुई है। भीषण गर्मी और तेज लू के कारण लोग बीमार हो रहे हैं। उन्होंने कहा कि लू से बचने का सबसे बड़ा बचाव परहेज ही है।
